description: Composable DSP, UI, analysis, and corpus-extraction primitives for agentically assembling plugin-suite products without making any sample suite the framework identity.

researchSources.title: Digital Dynamic Range Compressor Design - A Tutorial and Analysis
Reducing the Aliasing of Nonlinear Waveshaping Using Continuous-Time Convolution
Virtual Analog Modeling of Audio Circuitry Using Wave Digital Filters

equalization.title: Equalization
equalization.auditFinding: EQ should be represented as a band topology plus direct graph manipulation, not as unrelated frequency/gain/Q sliders.
sophisticationPath: Start with stable RBJ-style biquad bands and clear control roles.
Promote bands into graph entities with filter kind, gain, frequency, Q, slope, and bypass as one editable unit.
Add dynamic/spectral behavior by attaching sidechain detectors and analyzer probes to individual bands.
Validate with magnitude-response sweeps, phase/latency expectations, and band-handle UI parity.

compression.title: Compression
compression.auditFinding: Dynamics plugins need explicit detector, gain-computer, ballistics, transfer-curve, and metering primitives so style changes are controlled design choices.
sophisticationPath: Start with a feed-forward topology, threshold/ratio/knee, and attack/release smoothing.
Model sidechain filtering, detector domain, and timing as separate agent-visible choices.
Expose transfer curves and gain-history surfaces as primary editors instead of hiding behavior behind slider banks.
Validate with step response, tone-burst recovery, crest-factor material, and gain-reduction trace assertions.

saturation.title: Saturation
saturation.auditFinding: Saturation needs a declared nonlinearity, anti-aliasing strategy, tone-shaping context, and harmonic-analysis harness before it can be agentically tuned.
sophisticationPath: Start with a bounded memoryless shaper and gain staging.
Add oversampling or antiderivative-style anti-aliasing requirements when generated harmonics can exceed Nyquist.
Upgrade character models with multiband routing, pre/post EQ, feedback context, or wave-digital virtual-analog stages.
Validate with harmonic spectra, alias energy, loudness-normalized nulls, and intermodulation probes.

space.title: Space and Reverb
space.auditFinding: Reverb products need macro room geometry, decay contour, damping, and post-shaping primitives that can be staged from impulse and decay probes.
sophisticationPath: Start with room size, decay, damping, and mix as a macro field.
Add decay-rate EQ and post-EQ as direct visual surfaces.
Validate with impulse response, decay slope, stereo width, and freeze/gate probes.

delay.title: Delay
delay.auditFinding: Delay products need tap timing, feedback topology, tone shaping, and modulation primitives rather than disconnected time/mix sliders.
sophisticationPath: Start with tempo-aware taps and feedback gain.
Add feedback-path filtering, diffusion, and modulation slots.
Validate with impulse repeats, feedback stability, and modulation sideband probes.

instrument.title: Instrument Voice
instrument.auditFinding: Synth products need oscillator, filter, envelope, performance, and modulation primitives that expose voice architecture directly.
sophisticationPath: Start with oscillator, filter, envelope, amplifier, and output primitives.
Add modulation routing, voice allocation, and performance surfaces.
Validate with note-on envelopes, tuning, aliasing, and modulation-depth probes.

spatial.title: Spatial and Channel Utility
spatial.auditFinding: Channel utility plugins expose reusable routing, polarity, width, pan, delay compensation, and stereo-analysis primitives that should not be tied to one suite.
sophisticationPath: Start with gain, polarity, channel swap, pan, width, and delay compensation roles.
Add M/S encode-decode and L/R versus M/S manipulation as explicit topology choices.
Validate with null tests, correlation traces, channel delay probes, and matrix round-trips.

phase.title: Phase Alignment
phase.auditFinding: Mic, cabinet, console, and parallel-processing workflows often need all-pass rotation and phase-alignment primitives rather than simple polarity flips.
sophisticationPath: Start with polarity, sample delay, and first-order all-pass rotation as separate controls.
Add frequency-selective phase rotation, mono compatibility views, and null-depth feedback.
Validate with opposed-channel sweeps, group-delay traces, and comb-filter residual probes.

metering.title: Metering and Analysis
metering.auditFinding: Analysis plugins are framework primitives in their own right: FFT, spectrograph, phase, loudness, scope, and tuner views should feed staging and agent evaluation.
sophisticationPath: Start with peak/RMS and gain-reduction meters.
Add FFT, spectrogram, phase, loudness-history, scope, and tuner surfaces as reusable analyzers.
Validate by feeding known tones, phase offsets, pink noise, and loudness-calibrated material.

pitch.title: Pitch, Time, and Modulated Feedback
pitch.auditFinding: Pitch-shifting and creative feedback effects need matrix-style routing, modulation-source, pitch-display, and stability primitives.
sophisticationPath: Start with pitch shift, delay, filter, feedback, and mix blocks as reorderable topology nodes.
Add drawable modulators, envelope followers, MIDI pitch control, and preset sequencing.
Validate with pitch-tracking tones, feedback stability guards, modulation sidebands, and latency probes.

analog.title: Analog Color and Console Stages
analog.auditFinding: Vintage plug-ins frequently combine subtle transformer, tube, op-amp, impedance, and console-path behavior with otherwise simple gain or EQ controls.
sophisticationPath: Start with calibrated input/output gain, polarity, pad, and impedance roles.
Add level-dependent harmonic fingerprints, noise, crosstalk, hum, and frequency-dependent coloration.
Validate with level sweeps, stepped gain staging, harmonic residuals, null tests, and channel-correlation probes.

tape.title: Magnetic Tape and Recorder Transport
tape.auditFinding: Tape models bundle saturation, head bump, bias, tape speed, wow/flutter, calibration, hiss, crosstalk, and transport behavior into one sonic primitive family.
sophisticationPath: Start with tape speed, input, output, bias, and noise roles.
Add frequency-dependent saturation, head bump, hysteresis-like memory, wow/flutter, and repro/record calibration.
Validate with sine-level sweeps, low-frequency head bump scans, wow/flutter modulation, hiss spectra, and transient smear probes.

amp.title: Amplifier, Cabinet, and Mic Chain
amp.auditFinding: Guitar/bass amp references are whole signal chains: preamp, tone stack, power stage, speaker/cabinet, microphone, room, and impedance interactions.
sophisticationPath: Start with gain stages, tone stack, cabinet IR, and mic placement roles.
Add dynamic power-stage sag, speaker resonance, transformer color, and room/mic blend.
Validate with stepped drive sweeps, chord intermodulation, cabinet impulse response, and touch-response envelopes.

modulation.title: Vintage Delay and Modulation
modulation.auditFinding: Chorus, flanger, tape echo, BBD, and digital delay classics share time-varying delay, feedback, clock color, noise, and filtering primitives.
sophisticationPath: Start with delay time, depth, rate, feedback, mix, and tone roles.
Add BBD/tape/early-digital bandwidth limits, companding, clock noise, wow/flutter, and stereo phase offsets.
Validate with impulse repeats, modulation sidebands, feedback stability, noise floor, and stereo decorrelation probes.

microphone.title: Microphone and Recording Chain Modeling
microphone.auditFinding: Mic modeling references expose source mic, target mic, proximity, axis, room, and preamp-chain behavior that should become reusable profiling surfaces.
sophisticationPath: Start with source/target model selection, proximity, axis, and output roles.
Add capsule resonance, polar behavior, room/mic blend, and preamp coupling.
Validate with response curves, proximity sweeps, polar snapshots, and transient coloration probes.

eq.parametric-band.title: Parametric Band
eq.parametric-band.description: A cohesive EQ band entity with filter type, frequency, gain, Q/bandwidth, slope, and bypass represented together.
eq.parametric-band.dspIntent: Use stable filter coefficients or Faust filter primitives while preserving boost/cut reciprocity and predictable bandwidth mapping.
agentDesignNotes: Bind frequency/gain/Q as one surface-owned band before adding fallback dock controls.
Prefer typed band descriptors over independent slider sections.

eq.dynamic-band.title: Dynamic or Spectral Band
eq.dynamic-band.description: An EQ band whose gain or selectivity responds to detector energy in a focused spectral region.
eq.dynamic-band.dspIntent: Attach sidechain detection and bounded gain movement to a filter band while keeping static EQ response inspectable.
agentDesignNotes: Represent static and dynamic curves separately so agents can compare what the band is versus what it is doing.
Require a detector probe before claiming spectral or adaptive behavior.

eq.filterbank-crossover.title: Filterbank or Crossover
eq.filterbank-crossover.description: A split/recombine structure for multiband EQ, dynamics, and saturation products.
eq.filterbank-crossover.dspIntent: Use complementary band-splits or clearly documented crossover behavior so band routing remains phase-aware.
agentDesignNotes: Use this primitive whenever a product claims multiband behavior.
Do not model split frequencies as generic sliders when the region editor can own them.

compression.feedforward-sidechain.title: Feed-Forward Sidechain
compression.feedforward-sidechain.description: A predictable dynamics topology with a sidechain level detector driving a gain computer and smoothing stage.
compression.feedforward-sidechain.dspIntent: Separate detector, gain computer, smoothing, makeup, and gain stage so topology/style choices stay inspectable.
agentDesignNotes: Treat compression style as a topology preset over detector and ballistics, not a cosmetic enum.
Always pair the transfer curve with a time-domain gain-reduction trace.

compression.detector-ballistics.title: Detector Ballistics
compression.detector-ballistics.description: Peak/RMS/log-domain detection plus attack, hold, release, lookahead, and linking rules.
compression.detector-ballistics.dspIntent: Make memory and smoothing explicit because dynamics behavior is nonlinear and time-dependent.
agentDesignNotes: Surface sidechain filters visually; they are part of the compressor's sound, not secondary settings.
Use meter history to explain ballistics rather than relying on numeric attack/release controls.

compression.multiband-dynamics.title: Multiband Dynamics Region
compression.multiband-dynamics.description: A crossover-owned region that applies compression, expansion, or gating behavior within a frequency band.
compression.multiband-dynamics.dspIntent: Compose filterbank crossover, detector ballistics, and gain computer primitives per band.
agentDesignNotes: Band regions should own crossover and threshold gestures together.
Expose compression versus expansion mode as a region behavior, not a separate product concept.

saturation.memoryless-waveshaper.title: Memoryless Waveshaper
saturation.memoryless-waveshaper.description: A static nonlinear transfer function with explicit drive, bias, symmetry, and output compensation.
saturation.memoryless-waveshaper.dspIntent: Apply bounded nonlinear mapping with visible transfer curve and gain staging.
agentDesignNotes: A drive slider without a declared curve and output strategy is not enough to describe saturation.
Pair every nonlinear transfer with harmonic analysis probes.

saturation.antialiasing-strategy.title: Anti-Aliasing Strategy
saturation.antialiasing-strategy.description: Oversampling, continuous-time convolution, or antiderivative-style mitigation for nonlinear stages.
saturation.antialiasing-strategy.dspIntent: Constrain alias energy generated by nonlinear processing and declare the cost/quality tradeoff.
agentDesignNotes: Any product with aggressive drive should carry an anti-aliasing primitive.
Expose quality/latency explicitly so generated plugins can be staged and compared.

saturation.virtual-analog-stage.title: Virtual-Analog Stage
saturation.virtual-analog-stage.description: A circuit-inspired nonlinear block whose topology, feedback context, and component roles are explicit.
saturation.virtual-analog-stage.dspIntent: Use wave-digital, zero-delay-feedback, or carefully bounded circuit-inspired models when character claims exceed static waveshaping.
agentDesignNotes: Use this primitive when the app claims analog character, tube/diode behavior, or nonlinear filter feedback.
Require stability and aliasing probes before making the stage a flagship feature.

saturation.multiband-drive.title: Multiband Drive
saturation.multiband-drive.description: A band-split saturation workstation with per-band style, drive, tone, dynamics, and modulation hooks.
saturation.multiband-drive.dspIntent: Compose crossover and saturation stages so each band can be staged, metered, and auditioned independently.
agentDesignNotes: Use the multiband editor as the primary control surface, with fallback controls only for non-gestural details.
Treat modulation targets as part of the primitive so agents can wire movement consistently.

space.reverb-macro-field.title: Reverb Macro Field
space.reverb-macro-field.description: A room-shaping primitive that groups size, decay, damping, brightness, width, and freeze/gate behavior as one spatial editor.
space.reverb-macro-field.dspIntent: Expose perceptual room controls while keeping decay contour and post-shaping inspectable.
agentDesignNotes: Represent room shape as a field of linked nodes rather than isolated room-size and decay sliders.
Pair every reverb macro with impulse-response inspection.

delay.tap-feedback-network.title: Tap and Feedback Network
delay.tap-feedback-network.description: A tempo-aware delay primitive with tap placement, feedback routing, filtering, diffusion, and modulation slots.
delay.tap-feedback-network.dspIntent: Make time, feedback, filter, and modulation behavior visible as one repeat network.
agentDesignNotes: Use a timeline as the primary editor for delay time and tap relationships.
Treat feedback filtering and modulation as part of the delay network, not separate utility sections.

instrument.oscillator-filter-voice.title: Oscillator Filter Voice
instrument.oscillator-filter-voice.description: A subtractive voice primitive that binds oscillator color, filter shaping, envelope timing, performance, and output behavior.
instrument.oscillator-filter-voice.dspIntent: Expose the playable voice path from oscillator through filter and amplitude envelope with visible modulation hooks.
agentDesignNotes: Represent the synth as a voice path and modulation system, not a generic parameter grid.
Keep performance state visible so generated instruments can be staged interactively.

eq.circuit-model-topology.title: Circuit Model EQ Topology
eq.circuit-model-topology.description: An EQ band or curve family that exposes model/subtype choices, symmetry behavior, phase mode, grouping, and graph interaction preferences.
eq.circuit-model-topology.dspIntent: Separate band math from model topology so analog-inspired curves, symmetric/asymmetric behavior, and phase variants become explicit choices.
agentDesignNotes: Use this when a reference exposes named EQ models, symmetric/asymmetric curves, or subtype menus.
Keep model choice declarative so agents can swap topology without rewriting graph interactions.

compression.true-peak-limiter.title: True-Peak or Multistage Limiter
compression.true-peak-limiter.description: A limiting primitive with lookahead, transient handling, release strategy, ceiling, oversampling, and loudness/true-peak analysis hooks.
compression.true-peak-limiter.dspIntent: Make peak capture, inter-sample safety, release behavior, and loudness impact explicit rather than treating a limiter as a compressor with high ratio.
agentDesignNotes: Declare whether transient and sustained limiting are separate stages.
Pair limiter claims with true-peak and loudness probes in the staging harness.

compression.split-band-focus.title: Split-Band Detector Focus
compression.split-band-focus.description: A de-essing, gating, or expansion primitive that separates detector band, processed band, audition routing, and difference monitoring.
compression.split-band-focus.dspIntent: Let detector EQ, split filters, noise/sibilance classifiers, and listen modes shape dynamics behavior independently from output gain.
agentDesignNotes: Expose what is detected separately from what is processed.
Use listen/difference routing as a core primitive for agentic tuning.

spatial.channel-toolkit.title: Channel Toolkit
spatial.channel-toolkit.description: A stereo utility primitive for gain, polarity, channel swap, M/S encode-decode, width, pan, output selection, and delay compensation.
spatial.channel-toolkit.dspIntent: Represent channel routing as a reversible matrix with explicit monitoring and null-test expectations.
agentDesignNotes: Use this primitive before inventing per-product channel utility controls.
Validate matrix operations with null and round-trip probes.

phase.all-pass-alignment-network.title: All-Pass Phase Alignment Network
phase.all-pass-alignment-network.description: A frequency-dependent phase-rotation primitive for mic alignment, cabinet blending, parallel processing, and polarity-aware tone correction.
phase.all-pass-alignment-network.dspIntent: Model phase alignment as controllable all-pass rotation plus delay and polarity context instead of a binary invert switch.
uiExtractionSignals: phase rotation, delay, polarity, and alignment controls
null or correlation meter near alignment controls
mic/cabinet blend workflows with phase correction
agentDesignNotes: Do not collapse phase alignment into a generic stereo width control.
Capture group delay and null depth so agentic fitting can distinguish tone coloration from alignment.

metering.analysis-suite.title: Analysis Suite
metering.analysis-suite.description: A collection of FFT, octave, spectrograph, phase, scope, loudness, RMS, tuner, and history analyzers as reusable staging surfaces.
metering.analysis-suite.dspIntent: Make analysis views part of the framework so agents can inspect plugin behavior visually and sonically in host/stage contexts.
agentDesignNotes: Treat analyzer surfaces as reusable development instrumentation, not merely product UI.
Route sonic staging outputs through this primitive for screenshot and metric capture.

pitch.modulated-feedback-shifter.title: Modulated Feedback Pitch Shifter
pitch.modulated-feedback-shifter.description: A creative matrix primitive combining pitch shift, splice, delay, filtering, feedback, drawable modulators, envelope following, and MIDI pitch control.
pitch.modulated-feedback-shifter.dspIntent: Represent pitch/time feedback as a topology graph whose stability, modulation, and pitch display can be validated.
agentDesignNotes: Use matrix topology rather than a flat control list for pitch/time feedback effects.
Require stability guards for feedback paths before native staging.

analog.preamp-console-stage.title: Vintage Preamp or Console Stage
analog.preamp-console-stage.description: A level-dependent analog input/output stage covering transformer, tube, transistor, op-amp, impedance, crosstalk, hum, and noise color.
analog.preamp-console-stage.dspIntent: Model gain staging as a nonlinear calibrated circuit path rather than a transparent trim control.
uiExtractionSignals: input/output trims grouped around a preamp module
VU or clip meter near gain stage
agentDesignNotes: Prefer calibrated stage descriptors over generic Drive controls.
Track input/output level separately so emulation fitting can distinguish color from loudness.

tape.magnetic-recorder-stage.title: Magnetic Tape Recorder Stage
tape.magnetic-recorder-stage.description: Tape transport primitive with tape speed, formula, bias, calibration, head bump, saturation, noise, wow/flutter, and record/repro coloration.
tape.magnetic-recorder-stage.dspIntent: Represent tape as a memory-bearing recorder path with frequency-dependent saturation and transport modulation.
uiExtractionSignals: tape speed, formula, bias, calibration, repro/record controls
agentDesignNotes: Separate transport modulation from saturation.
Fit tape noise and head bump as explicit submodels instead of baking them into one waveshaper.

compression.vintage-compressor-model.title: Vintage Compressor Model
compression.vintage-compressor-model.description: A model-aware compressor primitive for FET, opto, vari-mu, diode bridge, VCA, and transient-shaper behavior coupled to analog color.
compression.vintage-compressor-model.dspIntent: Treat compressor model as detector topology, gain-cell memory, timing behavior, and coloration, not as a style label.
uiExtractionSignals: named model selector or legacy unit faceplate
agentDesignNotes: Infer model family before fitting timing constants.
Pair dynamics envelope loss with harmonic/color residual loss.

eq.passive-vintage-program-eq.title: Passive Vintage Program EQ
eq.passive-vintage-program-eq.description: A broad musical EQ primitive for passive, inductor/capacitor, stepped-frequency, boost/attenuate, and interacting-band equalizers.
eq.passive-vintage-program-eq.dspIntent: Model interaction between boost, attenuation, bandwidth, and passive makeup amplification instead of independent textbook bands.
uiExtractionSignals: passive EQ or program EQ language
agentDesignNotes: Do not approximate passive program EQs as independent peaking filters unless the interaction error is measured.
Expose stepped controls as topology data for Faust generation.

space.mechanical-room-reverb.title: Mechanical, Chamber, and Vintage Digital Reverb
space.mechanical-room-reverb.description: A reverb primitive for springs, plates, chambers, rooms, and early digital algorithms with mic placement, damping, modulation, and decay coloration.
space.mechanical-room-reverb.dspIntent: Represent reverb as an impulse/decay system with source-room-mic geometry or vintage algorithm controls.
agentDesignNotes: Store IR-derived fingerprints separately from macro controls.
Use room/mic surfaces when references expose studio/chamber placement.

modulation.vintage-delay-modulation.title: Vintage Delay, Chorus, Flanger, and Ensemble
modulation.vintage-delay-modulation.description: A time-modulation primitive for tape echo, BBD chorus/flanger, early digital delay, ensemble, doubler, and spatial modulation units.
modulation.vintage-delay-modulation.dspIntent: Model modulated delay as delay-line topology plus bandwidth, noise, feedback, companding, clocking, and stereo phase behavior.
uiExtractionSignals: echo heads or delay time controls
agentDesignNotes: Fit delay topology before fitting color.
Separate LFO shape, delay-line color, and feedback filtering.

amp.cabinet-mic-chain.title: Amp, Cabinet, Speaker, and Mic Chain
amp.cabinet-mic-chain.description: A full instrument amplification chain primitive covering preamp drive, tone stack, power amp, speaker cabinet, mic placement, room, and output gain.
amp.cabinet-mic-chain.dspIntent: Model amp products as cascaded nonlinear and convolutional blocks whose response changes with level and playing dynamics.
agentDesignNotes: Keep cabinet/mic convolution separate from nonlinear amp stages.
Profile with program material, not only sine waves.

instrument.electromechanical-keyboard.title: Electromechanical Keyboard or Organ
instrument.electromechanical-keyboard.description: A playable instrument primitive for piano, electric piano, organ, drawbar, rotary speaker, key noise, release, and pickup/body resonance models.
instrument.electromechanical-keyboard.dspIntent: Represent sampled/modelled keys as excitation, resonator/body, noise, articulation, performance, and effects modules.
agentDesignNotes: Model performance and articulation as primitives, not static synth controls.
Tie UI profiling to MIDI-driven sonic probes.

microphone.modeling-chain.title: Microphone Modeling Chain
microphone.modeling-chain.description: A microphone modeling primitive covering source mic, target mic, proximity, axis, polar response, room, and preamp coupling.
microphone.modeling-chain.dspIntent: Treat mic modeling as a capture-to-target transform with frequency, transient, phase, proximity, and polar behavior.
agentDesignNotes: Keep model transforms declarative so target models can be compared.
Capture source assumptions explicitly in emulation specs.

meaning.observed: Seen in an outside reference or sample suite and captured as feature evidence.
meaning.modeled: Represented as framework metadata with DSP intent, control roles, surface roles, and analysis probes.
meaning.implemented: Has a reusable DSP, schema, or preview implementation path in the framework.
meaning.surface-bound: Resolved into generated schemas and exercised by preview surface regressions.
meaning.sonically-verified: Backed by automated sonic probes that check characteristic behavior.
meaning.native-ready: Validated in native plugin builds and staging hosts.
